fields.icd_description: Fracture of neck of right femur, initial encounter
fields.icd_conflict_detail: Minor typographic discrepancy in ICD code formatting between the referral form ('S72.001A') and clinical notes/prescription ('S72.001A'); all documents agree on the same diagnosis description. Clinical notes and prescription confirmed as authoritative, resolving to S72.001A.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: All three documents consistently reference S72.001A with the same diagnosis description; minor formatting variation noted on the referral form but all sources align on the same code and clinical notes/prescription confirm S72.001A as correct.

fields.priority_reason: Acute femoral neck fracture (initial encounter) is a serious orthopedic injury with high fall and re-injury risk; the physician explicitly notes the patient is at significant risk of re-injury or clinical deterioration without the prescribed forearm crutches, warranting urgent equipment fulfillment.
fields.notes: 1) Authorization Reference Number is missing from the referral form — adjuster Diana Cruz should be contacted at (860) 555-0143 or d.cruz@hartford.com to obtain prior authorization before fulfillment. 2) Physician NPI is blank on all three documents; NPI should be verified and obtained from Desert Orthopedics before billing. 3) Clinical notes describe expected duration as 'Indefinite — chronic condition', which is atypical for an initial encounter fracture diagnosis and may warrant clarification with the physician. 4) Patient BMI appears elevated (5'3" / 193 lbs — BMI ~34); verify that the selected forearm crutch model supports the patient's weight capacity. 5) Referral submitted 08/08/2026, 15 days post-injury; confirm whether urgent delivery is required. 6) Prescription is valid through 11/06/2026 (90 days from 08/08/2026).

fired_rules.source: CMS DME MAC Jurisdiction D — Prior Authorization Requirements
fired_rules.message: Workers compensation claim — authorization reference number required for E0143 billing. Missing auth ref blocks claim submission.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear Diana Cruz,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding a pending durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your patient, **Daniel Johnson**, under claim number **WC-2026-084443**.

Upon reviewing the referral documentation, we have identified the following items that are required to move forward with processing:

- **Authorization Reference Number (auth_ref)** *(Hard Block – routing is on hold pending receipt)*
- **Prescribing Physician NPI (physician_npi)**

Please note that the missing authorization reference number is currently preventing us from routing this referral. We are unable to proceed until this information is received.

To avoid any delay in care for your patient, we kindly ask that you provide the outstanding information **within 24 business hours** of receiving this message. You may reply directly to this email with the required details.

If you have any questions or need assistance, please do not hesitate to reach out.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Best regards,
**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME
